_metadata.source: Hopkins Corridor Project — City of Berkeley workshop presentations and staff report
_metadata.status: VERIFIED — numbers sourced directly from city documents

primary_documents.title: Hopkins Corridor Project Conceptual Design — Council Staff Report
Workshop 4.3 — McGee to Gilman Near-Term Recommendations
Hopkins West of Gilman — Virtual Community Meeting

notes: East-of-Gilman segment-level space counts not in text portions of documents — require design map images (pages 8-20 of staff report). Those fields marked null.
West-of-Gilman parking inventory IS explicitly stated in December 2022 meeting document.
The 30-35 and 60 space estimates refer to east-of-Gilman only.
December 2022 document covers a separate western extension not yet approved.

collision_data_from_workshops.note: Corridor-wide collision stats cited in Workshop 4.2 and 4.3 — injury and fatal collisions only
collision_data_from_workshops.source: SWITRS 2016-2019, cited in city workshop presentations
collision_data_from_workshops.key_finding: Every reported collision involving a pedestrian or cyclist resulted in an injury or fatality
collision_data_from_workshops.caveat: This 18-collision figure is the injury/fatal subset only. The full TIMS dataset shows 51 total collisions in the corridor. The 78% figure applies to the injury/fatal subset.

east_of_gilman.description: Segments covered by the May 2022 Council-approved design (Sutter to Gilman)

original_estimate_lost.source: Early city estimate — disclosed before May 2022 Council vote

revised_estimate_lost.source: Revised staff estimate — not disclosed to Council or public before May 2022 vote. Revealed October 2022 by Councilmember Hahn.

segments.label: Sutter St to The Alameda
segments.character: Residential — single-family homes, North Branch Library at The Alameda
segments.design_images_caption: Proposed treatment — Sutter St to The Alameda (east to west). Source: Hopkins Corridor Project Conceptual Design, May 2022.

segments.label: The Alameda to McGee Ave

proposed_design.parking: Most on-street parking retained both sides
proposed_design.loading: Buffer zone provides separation and loading area

segments.note: Exact count not in text portions retrieved — requires design map images
segments.source: Staff report page 3; Workshop 4.2 presentation March 7 2022
segments.design_images_caption: Proposed treatment — The Alameda to McGee Ave (east to west). Source: Hopkins Corridor Project Conceptual Design, May 2022.

segments.label: McGee Ave to Monterey Ave

proposed_design.southside: Bi-directional bikeway against curb, protected by parked vehicles
proposed_design.parking: All parking retained on south side except one stall
proposed_design.loading: Buffer zone for separation and loading

segments.design_images_caption: Proposed treatment — McGee Ave to Monterey Ave. Source: Workshop 4.3, March 14 2022.
segments.design_images_note: Images cover the full commercial strip McGee to Gilman; first image assigned to this sub-segment by east-to-west order.

segments.label: Monterey Ave to Gilman St
segments.character: Commercial strip core — most contested blocks

proposed_design.protection: Raised concrete median separates drivers from cyclists

segments.note: All parking removed — exact pre-existing count requires design map images
segments.fatality_note: 2017 pedestrian fatality at Hopkins/Monterey — raised crosswalk proposed at this intersection
segments.source: Staff report pages 3-4; Workshop 4.3 presentation March 14 2022
segments.design_images_caption: Proposed treatment — Monterey Ave to Gilman St. Source: Workshop 4.3, March 14 2022.
segments.design_images_note: Images cover the full commercial strip McGee to Gilman; later two images assigned to this sub-segment by east-to-west order.

locations.direction_change: Made one-way southbound to accommodate angled parking

replacement_parking.net_commercial_strip_source: Berkeleyside reporting — 'net loss of five spots' after replacement. Verify against design maps.

west_of_gilman.description: Western extension options presented December 2022 — not approved
west_of_gilman.key_finding: Peak occupancy is 60% — 40% of spaces are empty even at peak. Option 3 (maximum extension) would displace ~70 vehicles during peak against a backdrop of 62 empty spaces at that same moment.
west_of_gilman.status: No option approved — project postponed April 2023

design_images.source: Screenshots from Hopkins Corridor Project Conceptual Design PDF (May 2022) and Workshop 4.3 (March 14 2022). Uploaded to Cloudinary March 2026.
design_images.commercial_strip_image_note: Three body images provided for full McGee-to-Gilman section without sub-segment attribution. Distributed by east-to-west order: first to mcgee-monterey, remaining two to monterey-gilman. Monterey intersection image shared between both sub-segments.
